title: The Smell of Your Perfume
content: Memory has a curious way of ignoring calendars. Years can pass without warning, yet a single familiar scent is enough to erase the distance between yesterday and today. It happened to me while walking through a crowded bookstore. Someone passed by wearing the same perfume you used to wear, and suddenly I was no longer surrounded by strangers. I was back in our small apartment, where Sunday mornings began with coffee, quiet music, and conversations that wandered without purpose. I remembered how you would leave your scarf on the chair and laugh whenever I complained about finding your books in every room. None of those details seemed important while we were living them. Only later did I understand that love often hides inside ordinary habits. The perfume disappeared as quickly as it had arrived, but the memories remained. They no longer carried sadness. Instead, they reminded me that some people leave invisible traces in our lives, impossible to see yet impossible to erase.
